title: True or False? — Tnufa A2 facts
subtitle: 10 statements about the Tnufa English Grade 9 exam (Track A-C)

items.text: The Tnufa English Grade 9 exam tests CEFR level A2 (Basic User).
items.explanation: True. The Table of Specifications (Ramah, 2026) states the exam is aligned with CEFR A2 — "Basic User".

items.text: Tnufa English is 90 minutes long, with 30 extra minutes for students with accommodations.
items.explanation: True. The official exam duration is 90 minutes, with 30 additional minutes for students with documented needs.

items.text: Reading is 75% of the Tnufa English exam.
items.explanation: True. The exam breakdown: Vocabulary ~20%, Reading ~75%, Writing ~5%. Reading dominates the assessment.

items.text: Students may use AI tools during the Tnufa English exam.
items.explanation: Wrong. Unlike Tnufa Information Literacy, students may NOT use AI tools in the English exam. The English exam is closed-resource.

items.text: The Tnufa English exam may include up to 18 different text types.
items.explanation: True. The text types pool includes advertisement, message, notice, flyer, comic strip, instructions, letter, diary entry, blog, biography, story, recipe, brochure, timetable, article, review, interview, web page.

items.text: Tnufa English provides individual student scores.
items.explanation: Wrong. Like all Tnufa exams, results are reported only at the SCHOOL level — there are no individual student grades. Reports show distributions across proficiency levels.

items.text: The writing task in Tnufa English requires 40-50 words.
items.explanation: True. The writing section asks students to write one paragraph of 40-50 words, in one of 7 text types (article, blog, friendly letter, etc.).

items.text: Lexical chunks are tested in addition to single words.
items.explanation: True. The vocabulary section explicitly tests BOTH single words and lexical chunks (e.g., "because of", "in order to").

items.text: Tnufa is the same as the old Meitzav (מיצ"ב) exam.
items.explanation: Wrong. Tnufa replaced Meitzav in תשפ"ו (2025-2026). Tnufa is digital, includes inferential/creative thinking, and reports only at school level.

items.text: The exam is administered on paper booklets, not digitally.
items.explanation: Wrong. All Tnufa exams are FULLY DIGITAL — administered on smartphones or computers. No paper booklets.
